items.title: Docs — fill ~530 docstrings (contract/API first) + ADRs (pure text)

items.title: Graph fork removal, fully gated (per-selector compat matrix on 0A-5 fixtures, PG_GRAPH_VERSION usage inventory, ResearchStateV2 migration fixtures, repeated replays, rollback+deprecation)
items.evidence: The fork is fully intact and unmodified as a fork: src/polaris_graph/__init__.py:37 still routes via resolve('PG_GRAPH_VERSION') to v3/v2/v1; graph.py, graph_v2.py (34201B), graph_v3.py (34670B) all still present. The only change to graph_v2.py on the branch is the Phase 1C config swap (MAX_ITERATIONS = int(resolve('PG_V2_MAX_ITERATIONS')) replacing os.getenv), not fork removal. None of the five 3C gates exist: no per-selector compatibility matrix or 0A-5 replay fixtures (found selector *tests* exist e.g. tests/polaris_graph/test_m46_selector_no_bypass.py but these are pre-existing evidence-selector tests, unrelated to the graph-version fork); no PG_GRAPH_VERSION usage inventory (only two incidental doc mentions: docs/pipeline_audit_context/08_env_var_inventory.md and a failure-cases doc); no ResearchStateV2 saved-state migration fixtures; no repeated-replay harness per selector; no rollback/deprecation-window doc. Note: 0A-5 (the 'before' selector fixtures that 3C depends on) also do not appear under baseline/, so the 3C precondition itself is absent.
items.gap: The graph fork is untouched by any removal work and none of the five mandatory 3C gates have been produced. 3C unstarted; its 0A-5 fixture precondition is also absent.

result.area_summary: Phase 3 (docs + required CI + graph fork) has NOT been started. The Phase 1 worktree (branch chore/review-readiness-phase1) tips at c5448ec 'Phase 1C docs'; the entire commit series is Phase 0 (baseline/oracle/CI-report-only/notices) and Phase 1 (config governance + os.getenv->resolve migration). There is no Phase 3 commit. Concretely: (3A) zero docstring-fill work and zero ADRs — find for adr-*/decision-record/*/adr/* returns nothing, and the only docs added are Phase-0/1 review_readiness notes; (3B) python_ci.yml is still explicitly REPORT-ONLY with continue-on-error:true and an inline comment 'Remove when ratcheted to required (Phase 3)', no flakiness-policy/quarantine bar, no dependency hash-pinning (grep --hash= => 0), and no license/secret scanning (no pip-audit/bandit/gitleaks/detect-secrets/etc. in CI, pre-commit, or pyproject); (3C) the graph.py/graph_v2/graph_v3 fork is fully intact — __init__.py still routes on PG_GRAPH_VERSION, the only edit to graph_v2.py is the Phase-1C resolve() swap, and none of the five required gates (per-selector compatibility matrix on 0A-5 fixtures, PG_GRAPH_VERSION usage inventory, ResearchStateV2 migration fixtures, repeated replays, rollback+deprecation window) exist. The 0A-5 selector 'before' fixtures that 3C is gated on also do not appear under baseline/. All three Phase 3 items are MISSING. This is consistent with the plan ordering (Phase 3 is LAST) — work simply has not reached it yet. Relevant paths: /home/polaris/wt/phase1/.github/workflows/python_ci.yml, /home/polaris/wt/phase1/src/polaris_graph/__init__.py, /home/polaris/wt/phase1/src/polaris_graph/graph_v2.py, /home/polaris/wt/phase1/docs/review_readiness/, /home/polaris/wt/phase1/requirements.lock.

(untracked) in polaris_project and is NOT committed on chore/review-readiness-phase1 or -phase0 (git ls-files shows no worklist/rename file). result.area_summary: Phase 2 (compat gate + renames + checkpoint) has NOT STARTED as an executed workstream. Every apparent 'phase2' doc found (docs/md*_phase2_threat_model.md, .codex/PHASE2_GATES.md, .codex/I-gen-002-phase2/*) belongs to an unrelated older gate/generation initiative, not this review-readiness Phase 2. THE ONE SUBSTANTIVE ARTIFACT that exists is the rename worklist: /home/polaris/polaris_project/NAME_RENAME_WORKLIST_validated.tsv, and its counts match Plan V4 section 2B EXACTLY — 210 RENAME rows split 105 SAFE / 12 TEXT-ONLY / 45 FILE-RENAME / 32 NEEDS-ALIAS / 16 DOMAIN-REVIEW, plus 135 KEEP rows (81 KEEP + 4 KEEP-BUT-NOTED + 50 KEEP-NOTED). But that file is git-untracked ('??') in the main project dir and is NOT committed to either review-readiness branch. Verdicts: 2A (public-compatibility inventory) = MISSING (no artifact anywhere); 2B (execute renames) = PARTIAL (worklist exists and is correct, but uncommitted to the branch and ZERO of 210 renames executed — original symbols/files verified still present in src/); 2C (turn on checkpoint) = MISSING (checkpoint code pre-exists from old commit 2b42bc5, still defaults PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED='0', no Phase-2 change to enable it). graph_v2/v3 correctly out of scope (Plan defers to Phase 3). Net: the 210-name worklist is produced and validated; nothing downstream (inventory gate, rename execution, checkpoint enablement) has begun.

items.title: Secret-safe baseline manifest (baseline/manifest.json)
items.evidence: No baseline/manifest.json exists at the plan's path. `find` + `git ls-files` show no `baseline/` directory (only unrelated outputs/*/manifest.json and test fixtures). pyproject.toml references a `baseline` exclude dir that does not exist. The .gitignore has no entry for a baseline manifest location. The only structured baseline artifacts are prose docs (docs/review_readiness/baseline_findings.md, baseline_test_state.md). Those docs textually state config counts (694 vars, 352 PG_*, 56 secret-shaped, 'secrets stored as digests in the git-ignored baseline') and Python 3.11.10 / commit d76a0ea, but no machine-readable manifest.json with commit SHA + requirements.lock hash + model routing + seeds + commands + fixtures + per-secret SHA-256 digest+present-flag was produced.
items.gap: The core 0A-1 deliverable — a secret-safe baseline/manifest.json enumerating every field the plan lists (requirements.lock hash, OS, model routing, seeds, exact commands, fixtures, per-secret SHA-256 digest + present/absent flag) — was never created. Only narrative findings docs exist. The 'git-ignored baseline' the docs cite is not present in the worktree.

items.title: Isolated sandbox N-run with per-artifact SHA-256
items.evidence: baseline_findings.md describes a single read-only exercise of two live scenarios (THIN, SATURATED) at commit d76a0ea. There is no dedicated scratch output dir manifest, no record of the GitHub backup daemon being paused, and NO per-output-artifact SHA-256 hashes recorded. The only SHA-256 list found (.codex/I-ready-017/faithfulness_baseline_sha256.txt) hashes 10 source files, not run output artifacts.
items.gap: No isolated-sandbox N-run with per-artifact SHA-256 capture exists. The described run was a single exploratory pass that crashed (FileNotFoundError on hardcoded /workspace/outline_agent_wt path), not an N-run with hashed artifacts.

items.title: Non-flakiness N>=3 with governing RACE/faithfulness measurement statistically characterized, never quarantined
items.evidence: baseline_test_state.md records only N=2 test-suite runs (1343 passed / 104 failed / 103 errors, identical both times) — below the plan's N>=3, and this covers only unit tests, NOT the governing RACE/faithfulness measurement. The plan REQUIRES the governing RACE score + faithfulness verdicts be characterized with mean+spread+equivalence band over N repeats, and that inability to characterize is a BLOCKING stop. baseline_findings.md instead documents that the acceptance harness is 'NOT a trustworthy oracle yet': positive control FAILED (THIN fired 0 searches when it should fire search_more_evidence), possibly vacuous, cause 'currently indeterminate'. No RACE score mean/spread/equivalence band was ever computed (grep for 'RACE'/'equivalence band'/'mean...spread' in review_readiness returns nothing substantive).
items.gap: The governing measurement is neither characterized nor blocked-and-halted per the plan — it is left in an admitted-broken, indeterminate state (positive control failing) while later oracle work proceeded. This inverts the plan's absolute rule: the un-pinned oracle should have been a BLOCKING condition, but the initiative continued. N>=3 was not met for anything (only N=2 unit runs).

items.title: Written comparison protocol (regression vs noise)
items.evidence: grep for 'comparison protocol', 'regression vs noise', 'equivalence band' across docs/ returns no matches. docs/review_readiness/ contains only baseline_findings.md, baseline_test_state.md, gpu_fix_and_search_resolution.md, and search_diagnosis/. None defines the '"same" = RACE within band AND identical faithfulness verdict sets AND artifact-hash match' protocol the plan mandates.
items.gap: No written comparison protocol declaring what counts as regression vs noise exists. The demo_artifact_diff.py docstring gestures at 'byte-compare final observable outputs across a frozen trace' as a concept, but that is a toy demo, not the declared-before-any-change protocol document.

items.title: Characterize all 3 graph selectors (v1/v2/v3) as replay fixtures
items.evidence: No docs or fixtures in docs/review_readiness/ mention graph_v2/graph_v3/PG_GRAPH_VERSION or per-selector 'before' fixtures. grep -l for graph_v2/graph_v3/PG_GRAPH_VERSION/selector across docs/review_readiness/ returns nothing. Pre-existing replay dirs (src/polaris_v6/replay, tests/polaris_graph/replay) are unrelated and predate this branch.
items.gap: None of the three graph selectors were characterized on fixed inputs as replay fixtures. This 0A-5 deliverable was not started.

items.title: SHA-pin all 28 CI actions
items.evidence: Commit 325f90e SHA-pinned action refs across 11 workflow files. Verification: grep of .github/workflows finds 0 refs still on @vN tags (unpinned check returns empty) and 33 refs pinned to 40-hex SHAs across 31 `uses:` lines (some files reuse the same action). Commit message says 31 references; plan said 28 — coverage is complete (>= the plan target), so acceptance ('SHA-pin all') is met.

items.title: Fix third-party attribution
items.evidence: THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md created (5c78cc0) and hardened over 4 codex-review rounds (e51fe11, 3aa3a6e, 6c3ea00, d8cbc0a): documents STORM/FS-Researcher clean-room provenance with paper citations, states SPDX/license-header scan returns none, pins DRB-II uv.lock provenance (8 PyPI deps + editable root). Marked as 'evidence not proof' and scoped to S6 for full SBOM — appropriately honest and defensible.

items.title: Add pyproject.toml + CI in REPORT-ONLY mode
items.evidence: pyproject.toml added (0835b1a) with ruff/mypy/interrogate config, explicitly NOT duplicating pytest.ini to avoid changing test collection. .github/workflows/python_ci.yml is report-only: `continue-on-error: true`, every tool step suffixed `|| true`, header comments state it must NOT block until Phase 3. .pre-commit-config.yaml added with SHA-pinned repos (rev: 40-hex for ruff v0.6.9 and pre-commit-hooks v4.6.0). This is the show-Telus-first artifact and it meets the report-only acceptance exactly.

items.title: Tidy README / architecture
items.evidence: README.md rebranded to 'Deep Cove Research' (5c78cc0, +7 lines) — head shows new title/description. However architecture.md was NOT touched on this branch (git diff d76a0ea..HEAD -- architecture.md docs/architecture* is empty).
items.gap: Plan 0B says 'tidy README/architecture'. README got a light rebrand only; architecture.md received no tidy. The architecture half of the item is unaddressed.

items.title: Deterministic oracle (tests/oracle/cassette.py, llm_cassette.py) mapping to 0A
items.evidence: A cassette core was built and hardened over 6 commits (8f02acb..0c94d01): tests/oracle/cassette.py (record/replay, exactly-once, atomic, stable ids, terminal finalize), llm_cassette.py wiring the async OpenRouter LLM boundary, and 12 passing unit tests (pytest tests/oracle/test_cassette.py -> 12 passed). demo_artifact_diff.py proves the concept: byte-identical replay on a no-op, diff caught on a real behavior change. registry.py was also made lazy-init (43214a2..0780157) to fix the 23 import-time collection errors.
items.gap: The oracle is Layer-1 infrastructure + a toy 2-LLM-call demo only; it is NOT wired to the real acceptance harness (grep for 'acceptance' in tests/oracle/ returns nothing) and does not yet characterize the governing RACE/faithfulness measurement. It does not satisfy 0A-3's requirement to pin the governing oracle. Additionally the acceptance harness's own portability crash (identified prerequisite in baseline_findings.md item 1) is UNFIXED: acceptance_outline_agent.py still hardcodes /workspace/outline_agent_wt (lines 28, 281-282). So the oracle exists as a building block but has not been connected to prove any refactor byte-safe against the real pipeline. Note: registry.py is importable runtime code, changed in Phase 0 despite the plan's '0B touches NO importable runtime code' framing — justified as a portability fix but worth flagging as a deviation.

result.area_summary:
Phase 0 splits cleanly into two halves with very different completeness. The 0B zero-risk tidy-ups are essentially DONE and are the strongest, review-ready evidence: all CI action refs are SHA-pinned (0 unpinned, verified), a defensible THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES survived 4 codex rounds, and pyproject.toml + a genuinely report-only python_ci.yml (continue-on-error + `|| true` everywhere) + SHA-pinned pre-commit are in place. README got a light rebrand (architecture.md untouched → 0B-readme-arch PARTIAL).

The 0A reproducible-baseline half — the plan's stated starting point and the foundation every later 'we did not move the score' claim depends on — is largely NOT delivered as specified. There is NO baseline/manifest.json (0A-1 MISSING), no isolated N-run with per-artifact SHA-256 (0A-2 MISSING), no written comparison protocol (0A-4 MISSING), and no graph-selector v1/v2/v3 replay fixtures (0A-5 MISSING). Only prose findings docs exist. Most seriously against the plan's one inviolable rule: 0A-3 is WRONG — the governing RACE/faithfulness measurement was never statistically characterized (mean+spread+equivalence band); instead the acceptance harness is documented as broken (positive control fires 0 searches, cause 'indeterminate'), only N=2 unit-test runs were recorded (< N>=3), and the initiative proceeded rather than treating the un-pinnable oracle as the BLOCKING stop the plan demands.

The deterministic oracle (cassette.py/llm_cassette.py, 12 passing tests, byte-diff demo) is real and well-hardened but is Layer-1 infrastructure with a toy demo (PARTIAL): it is not wired to the real acceptance harness, does not pin the governing measurement, and the harness's own hardcoded-path crash remains unfixed. registry.py was made lazy-init — a runtime-code change inside a phase framed as touching no importable runtime code (justified as portability, flagged as a deviation). Net: the safe cosmetic spine is shippable; the baseline proof-of-safety that Phase 1 is supposed to depend on does not yet exist in the form the plan requires.

items.title: Govern + own ALL ~923 keys (classify + owner + precedence), gate = 100% classified + owned
items.evidence: Commit 1d9a719 added docs/review_readiness/config_governance.md + baseline/config_keys_classified.json (235KB, gitignored). The JSON classifies 1,044 runtime keys (superset of the plan's ~923 estimate; full surface = 1,644 keys / 2,592 read-sites). Each entry carries category (model-selection/secret/internal-flag/tuning-threshold/external-system/internal-other), read_sites, files, in_env, secret_shaped. Python check: 1044 keys, 100% classified into 6 categories.
items.gap: The gate demands 'each with an owner and documented precedence' + '100% classified + OWNED before a single key migrates'. Verified via json.load: 0 of 1,044 entries carry an owner OR a precedence field. Governance doc assigns only a BLANKET owner ('until per-key owners are assigned, the project owner is accountable for P1 model+secret keys; executor drains P2/P3') — this is not the per-key ownership the gate requires, and there is no documented per-key precedence order (CLI vs .env vs process-env vs default). Classification is complete; ownership + precedence are not. Migration (1C) proceeded before the own-gate was satisfied.

items.title: FULL-behavior characterization matrix per key: {unset,empty,valid,malformed} x {default/.env/process-env/CLI precedence} x {key-case} x {read-timing lazy vs snapshot} x {runtime type str/coerced/SecretStr}
items.evidence: Two test files. tests/test_settings_models.py (46 lines) covers the 12 model keys: unset==getenv-default, override honoured, empty-string==getenv, fresh-read reflects env change (timing), cached-instance-is-snapshot (timing). tests/test_config_registry.py (26 lines) covers all 871 registry keys but ONLY: resolve==os.getenv for {unset, override}, plus unregistered-key raises. Commit 1d9a719 notes the alias design 'CAUGHT a real case-sensitivity bug (PG_judge_model vs PG_JUDGE_MODEL)'.
items.gap: The matrix is largely NOT covered. Actually tested: unset + empty + valid-override default resolution (2-3 of the {value} axis) for the 871-key registry, plus lazy-vs-snapshot timing and case-sensitivity for the 12 model keys only. NOT tested for any key: malformed input; the .env / process-env / CLI precedence ordering axis (no .env or CLI layering is exercised at all — resolve() is pure os.getenv so precedence is untested by construction); key-case axis for the 871-key tail; read-timing for the 871 tail; runtime-type coercion (the int()/float()/=='1' wrappers live at call sites and are NOT characterized — a malformed 'abc' feeding int() would raise, uncharacterized); SecretStr runtime type (does not exist). So 1B proves byte-identical DEFAULT resolution (its stated aim in the doc) but not the full-behavior matrix the plan wording requires. grep for precedence|malformed|secretstr|import-snapshot|case-sensit test names in tests/ returns no dedicated config coverage.

items.title: Migrate ONE MODULE AT A TIME, acceptance harness after each module
items.evidence: First slices were per-module: 6d8a733 (nli_verifier, 1 file, 'first module'), f746662 (4 more modules, model keys). Then 166639a added config_defaults.py (871 keys) + resolve(). Then dd96ceb is a SINGLE commit migrating 832 os.getenv sites across 130 modules in one shot (author polaris-bot), described as 'AST-based codemod', +962/-832 lines. So the tail was a BULK codemod, not one-module-at-a-time.
items.gap: 1C says 'Migrate one module at a time... Acceptance harness after each module; any deviation from the 0A baseline -> stop.' The 832-site tail deviates: it is a bulk codemod across 130 modules in one commit. Verification was import-cleanliness (130/130), pytest --collect-only (11 pre-existing errors, no new), and characterization tests — NOT the acceptance_outline_agent.py harness. git log 6d8a733..c5448ec shows NO acceptance/harness/RACE mention in any Phase 1 migration commit body. The deviation is SCORE-SAFE by construction (resolve(k) == os.getenv(k, CONFIG_DEFAULTS[k]); coercion wrappers int()/float()/=='1' remain unchanged at call sites; registry default == exact call-site literal, test-proven over all 871 keys), and env names are preserved verbatim. But the plan's per-module acceptance-harness cadence was skipped in favor of static/import/collection equivalence proof.

items.title: SecretStr for secret keys, call-sites unwrapped
items.evidence: grep -rc SecretStr src/polaris_graph/ returns zero matches anywhere in the source tree. settings.py ModelSettings has no SecretStr fields. 6 real API-key secrets (EXA_API_KEY, OPENROUTER_API_KEY, OPEN_PAGERANK_API_KEY, PG_OPENALEX_API_KEY, SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY, SERPER_API_KEY, ZYTE_API_KEY) sit in the plaintext config_defaults.py CONFIG_DEFAULTS as plain str (empty-string defaults). config_governance.md explicitly DEFERS this: '114 secret-shaped sites -> a separate SecretStr pass (behavior-changing; needs its own design).'
items.gap: Secrets are NOT migrated to SecretStr — the requirement is entirely deferred. No plaintext secret VALUE leaks (all secret defaults are ''), so no security regression was introduced, and deferral is arguably correct since SecretStr wrapping/unwrapping is behavior-changing (violates the byte-identical-in-one-commit rule). But as a plan item it is unstarted: no SecretStr type, no design doc for the unwrap pass, and 114 secret sites still read as plain os.getenv.

items.title: Byte-identical value preserved (no value changed while moving it); conflicting defaults handled safely
items.evidence: config_conflicts.md (commit c5448ec) documents 20 PG_ keys read with DIFFERENT hardcoded defaults at different call sites, deliberately EXCLUDED from the resolve() registry (no single byte-identical value). Includes PG_FAITHFULNESS_NLI_THRESHOLD (0.65 vs 0.75) flagged FAITHFULNESS-CRITICAL, PG_MIN_EVIDENCE_PER_SECTION (3/5/8), PG_MIN_TOTAL_WORDS (0 vs 10000). The migrated keys are byte-identical by construction and correctly leave these conflicts untouched.
items.gap: The 20 conflicting-default keys (incl. a faithfulness-gate threshold that runs at two strictnesses depending on code path) are DOCUMENTED but UNRESOLVED — 'each needs a product decision'. This is a latent pre-existing inconsistency Phase 1 surfaced but did not fix; correctly out of the byte-identical migration, but it means the frozen-faithfulness invariant already has a path-dependent divergence (0.65 vs 0.75) that remains live. Also: 512 os.getenv reads across 133 src files remain unmigrated (secret/computed/conflicting/non-PG tail), and the doc's claimed 'CI grep bans new os.getenv outside settings.py' guard is NOT implemented (no such check in .github/ or .pre-commit-config.yaml).

result.area_summary: Phase 1 (central settings) landed a real, score-safe foundation but is materially short of Plan V4's stated acceptance on three of its own gates. WHAT'S SOLID: a typed settings.py with the 12 model-selection keys as the single source of truth (byte-identical, case-sensitive aliases), an auto-generated 871-key CONFIG_DEFAULTS registry with resolve(), and an 832-site AST codemod (dd96ceb) that routes non-secret PG_ reads through resolve() — byte-identical BY CONSTRUCTION (resolve(k)==os.getenv(k,default), coercion wrappers untouched at call sites, env names preserved, registry default==exact literal, test-proven over all 871 keys). This does not move the RACE score. WHERE IT FALLS SHORT OF THE PLAN: (1A) all 1,044 runtime keys are classified but 0 carry per-key owner/precedence — the gate demands '100% classified + OWNED with documented precedence before a single key migrates,' and migration proceeded on a blanket-owner note instead. (1B) the 'full-behavior' characterization is really byte-identical DEFAULT-resolution only: unset/empty/override for the 871 tail plus timing+case for the 12 model keys — the plan's precedence (.env/process-env/CLI), malformed-input, key-case-for-tail, coercion-type, and SecretStr axes are untested. (1C) the tail was a BULK codemod across 130 modules in one commit, not one-module-at-a-time, and the per-module acceptance_outline_agent.py harness was never run (equivalence proven via import + collection + characterization instead) — a safe-but-off-process deviation. SecretStr is entirely MISSING (zero occurrences in src; 6 API-key secrets sit as plain str in the registry; the whole secret pass is deferred). Phase 1 also surfaced but did NOT resolve 20 conflicting-default keys, one of them (PG_FAITHFULNESS_NLI_THRESHOLD 0.65 vs 0.75) a live faithfulness-gate divergence, and 512 os.getenv reads across 133 files remain unmigrated with the promised CI grep-guard unimplemented. Net: the committed changes appear byte-identical/score-safe, but the plan items are DONE-by-construction rather than DONE-by-the-acceptance-wording, and two required deliverables (per-key ownership+precedence, SecretStr) are absent.

items.title: Config governance — classify + own 923 keys, precedence
items.evidence: Substantive work exists on branch chore/review-readiness-phase1. baseline/config_keys_classified.json classifies 1044 runtime keys (categories: model-selection 35, secret 97, internal-flag 71, tuning-threshold 372, external/system 35, internal-other 434). docs/review_readiness/config_governance.md documents the surface, migration approach, and progress (commits 1d9a719, 166639a, dd96ceb, c5448ec). settings.py + config_defaults.py + tests/test_config_registry.py + tests/test_settings_models.py lock byte-identical resolve()==os.getenv over 871 keys. config_conflicts.md surfaces 20 conflicting-default keys incl. faithfulness-critical PG_FAITHFULNESS_NLI_THRESHOLD (0.65 vs 0.75).
items.gap: Acceptance is '100% classified + owned + precedence' — two of three legs unmet. OWNED: the classified JSON has 0/1044 records with an owner field (fields are only read_sites/files/in_env/pg/secret_shaped/category); the doc explicitly says 'until per-key owners are assigned, the project owner is accountable' — a blanket fallback, not the per-key ownership the plan requires (plan 1A: 'each with an owner'). PRECEDENCE: 0/1044 records carry a precedence field; precedence is only described narratively (os.getenv semantics) — no per-key precedence spec and the full Phase-1B matrix (default/.env/process-env/CLI × case × malformed × timing × type) is not locked (tests cover only unset/override/empty). Also key count classified is 1044/1644 (600 scripts-only keys excluded), not the plan's headline 923.

items.title: Public-compatibility inventory + supported-Python/install/state-migration/rollback doc
items.evidence: No artifact created by the initiative. The full file list touched by the 26 review-readiness commits (5c78cc0..HEAD) contains no public-compat inventory and no S2 doc. docs/supported_scope.md exists but was last modified 2026-07-07 by an unrelated initiative (I-deepfix-001, commit 2b42bc5), predates the 2026-07-18 review-readiness work, and covers query/template scope — not public-API compatibility, supported-Python, install, state-migration, or rollback. grep for public-compat/rollback/state-migration/supported-python across docs/review_readiness/ and docs/telus/ returns nothing relevant.
items.gap: Entire S2 deliverable absent: no dynamic-import/string-ref/saved-state/external-consumer inventory (plan 2A), and no supported-Python/install/state-migration/rollback document. Phase 2 rename/delete is gated on this and cannot proceed.

items.title: Test quality / flakiness policy
items.evidence: docs/review_readiness/baseline_test_state.md (commit e1bc3af) characterizes the suite: 16,501 tests collected, 23 modules fail to collect, a clean-subset run repeated 2x showing DETERMINISTIC results (1343 passed / 104 failed / 103 errors both runs, identical), concluding failures are real debt not flakiness. The import-time registry root cause was subsequently fixed (commits 43214a2, ca71c82, 7f21b61, 0780157).
items.gap: Acceptance is 'suite stable across N runs; flaky (non-governing) quarantined; bar defined' — this is a 2-run baseline diagnosis, not the policy. N=2 (plan minimum is N>=3), no quarantine list of flaky non-governing tests, no defined non-flakiness bar, no governing-measurement statistical characterization (mean/spread/equivalence band per plan 0A-3), and 104 assertion failures + 103 errors remain untriaged. No flakiness policy document exists.

items.title: Security / privacy review (threat model: API auth, crawler SSRF, prompt-injection, PII, log redaction, checkpoint retention)
items.evidence: No S4 artifact produced by the initiative. The review-readiness commit-range file list contains no security/threat-model doc. grep for threat-model/SSRF/prompt-injection/log-redaction/PII/checkpoint-retention across docs/review_readiness/ returns nothing. Pre-existing docs/m26_threat_model.md and the docs/md*_threat_model.md family were last touched 2026-07-07 by I-deepfix-001 (commit 2b42bc5), predate the initiative, and are per-run/module artifacts of a different workstream — not the S4 review with the plan's five required axes.
items.gap: Entire S4 deliverable absent: no threat-model doc covering API auth, crawler SSRF, prompt-injection, PII, log redaction, and checkpoint retention; no critical-findings resolution log. Gated 'before external exposure' and unmet.

items.title: Operational readiness (timeouts, retries, rate/cost limits, monitoring, runbooks, recovery)
items.evidence: No S5 artifact produced by the initiative. The review-readiness commit-range file list contains no operational-readiness checklist. grep for operational-readiness/retries/rate-limit/cost-limit/runbook/recovery across docs/review_readiness/ returns nothing. Pre-existing docs/runbook.md and docs/run_observability_monitoring.md were last modified 2026-07-07 by I-deepfix-001 (commit 2b42bc5), predate the initiative, and were neither created nor updated for it.
items.gap: Entire S5 deliverable absent: no consolidated checklist covering timeouts, retries, rate/cost limits, monitoring, runbooks, and recovery produced or validated for review-readiness. Gated 'before external exposure' and unmet.

items.title: Reproducible build (two builds hash-match, hash-pinned deps, locked env, SBOM)
items.evidence: Phase 0 added pyproject.toml, report-only CI, and requirements.lock (uv pip compile, version-pinned) — commits 0835b1a, and DRB-II lock provenance docs (6c3ea00, d8cbc0a). However: no SBOM anywhere (find for *sbom*/*.spdx*/*cyclonedx* returns nothing); requirements.lock has 0 '--hash=' lines (version-pinned, NOT hash-pinned); no CI or script performs a two-build hash-match / reproducibility check (grep of .github/ finds no reproducible/diffoscope/sbom job).
items.gap: Acceptance requires all four: two independent builds hash-match + hash-pinned deps + locked env + SBOM. Only a version-pinned lockfile exists (partial 'locked env'); the hash-pinned deps, the two-build hash-match proof, and the SBOM are all absent. 'Reproducible' cannot be claimed to Telus.

result.area_summary: Of the six scheduled reviewer-axis deliverables (S1-S6), only S1 has substantive committed work and even it is PARTIAL; S3 has a foundational baseline diagnosis (PARTIAL); S2, S4, S5, and S6 are entirely MISSING from the review-readiness initiative. Evidence base: the complete file list touched by the 26 review-readiness commits (git range 5c78cc0..HEAD on branch chore/review-readiness-phase1) contains only docs/review_readiness/{baseline_findings, baseline_test_state, config_conflicts, config_governance, gpu_fix_and_search_resolution, search_diagnosis/*} plus config code/tests — no S2/S4/S5/S6 artifact. Docs that superficially match (supported_scope.md, m26_threat_model.md, runbook.md, run_observability_monitoring.md) were all last modified 2026-07-07 by the unrelated I-deepfix-001 workstream (commit 2b42bc5) and predate the 2026-07-18 review-readiness work; none were created or updated for these deliverables. S1's classification artifact (baseline/config_keys_classified.json, 1044 keys) has zero per-key owner or precedence fields, so its 'owned + precedence' acceptance legs are unmet despite strong byte-identical migration work. The initiative is heavily concentrated on Phase 0 baseline + Phase 1 config migration; the scheduled deliverables table is almost untouched. Note the s2s3/S0-S5 commits in the log belong to a separate 'Research Planning Gate' initiative and are not these deliverables.
